tool.name: Liana Methods
tool.description: Ligand_receptor inference and local bivariate spatial metrics for single-cell or spatial data
tool.command: #if $adata.ext == 'h5mu': ln -s '$adata' 'input.h5mu' && #else: ln -s '$adata' 'input_anndata.h5ad' && #end if cat '$script_file' > '$hidden_output' && python '$script_file' >> '$hidden_output' && ls . >> '$hidden_output' && touch 'anndata_info.txt' && cat 'anndata_info.txt' @CMD_PRETTIFY_STDOUT@

configfiles.configfile:
@CMD_IMPORTS@ #if $adata.ext == 'h5mu': import mudata as mu input_data = mu.read_h5mu('input.h5mu') #if str($method.method) not in ['bivariate', 'inflow']: raise ValueError('MuData input is supported only by the bivariate and inflow methods in this Galaxy tool.') #end if #else: adata = sc.read_h5ad('input_anndata.h5ad') input_data = adata #end if
#if str($method.method) == 'rank_aggregate': li.method.rank_aggregate( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ expr_prop=$method.expr_prop, min_cells=$method.min_cells, #if str($method.groupby_pairs.groupby_pairs_selector) == 'custom': groupby_pairs=pd.read_csv('$method.groupby_pairs.pairs_file', sep='\t'), #end if #if str($method.base) != '': base=$method.base, #end if aggregate_method='$method.aggregate_method', #if str($method.consensus_opts.consensus_opts_selector) == 'custom': consensus_opts=$method.consensus_opts.opts, #end if return_all_lrs=$method.return_all_lrs, #if str($method.key_added) != '': key_added='$method.key_added', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if de_method='$method.de_method', #if str($method.n_perms) != '': n_perms=$method.n_perms, #end if seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, verbose=False, inplace=True)
#else if str($method.method) == 'cellchat': li.method.cellchat( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ expr_prop=$method.expr_prop, min_cells=$method.min_cells, @CMD_SUPP_COLUMNS@ return_all_lrs=$method.return_all_lrs, #if str($method.key_added) != '': key_added='$method.key_added', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if de_method='$method.de_method', #if str($method.n_perms) != '': n_perms=$method.n_perms, #end if seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, verbose=False, inplace=True)
#else if str($method.method) == 'cellphonedb': li.method.cellphonedb( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ expr_prop=$method.expr_prop, min_cells=$method.min_cells, @CMD_SUPP_COLUMNS@ return_all_lrs=$method.return_all_lrs, #if str($method.key_added) != '': key_added='$method.key_added', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if de_method='$method.de_method', #if str($method.n_perms) != '': n_perms=$method.n_perms, #end if seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, verbose=False, inplace=True)
#else if str($method.method) == 'connectome': li.method.connectome( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ expr_prop=$method.expr_prop, min_cells=$method.min_cells, @CMD_SUPP_COLUMNS@ return_all_lrs=$method.return_all_lrs, #if str($method.key_added) != '': key_added='$method.key_added', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if de_method='$method.de_method', seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, verbose=False, inplace=True)
#else if str($method.method) == 'natmi': li.method.natmi( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ expr_prop=$method.expr_prop, min_cells=$method.min_cells, @CMD_SUPP_COLUMNS@ return_all_lrs=$method.return_all_lrs, #if str($method.key_added) != '': key_added='$method.key_added', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if de_method='$method.de_method', seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, verbose=False, inplace=True)
#else if str($method.method) == 'logfc': li.method.logfc( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ expr_prop=$method.expr_prop, min_cells=$method.min_cells, @CMD_SUPP_COLUMNS@ #if str($method.base) != '': base=$method.base, #end if return_all_lrs=$method.return_all_lrs, #if str($method.key_added) != '': key_added='$method.key_added', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if de_method='$method.de_method', seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, verbose=False, inplace=True)
#else if str($method.method) == 'singlecellsignalr': li.method.singlecellsignalr( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ expr_prop=$method.expr_prop, min_cells=$method.min_cells, @CMD_SUPP_COLUMNS@ return_all_lrs=$method.return_all_lrs, #if str($method.key_added) != '': key_added='$method.key_added', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if de_method='$method.de_method', seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, verbose=False, inplace=True)
#else if str($method.method) == 'geometric_mean': li.method.geometric_mean( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ expr_prop=$method.expr_prop, min_cells=$method.min_cells, @CMD_SUPP_COLUMNS@ return_all_lrs=$method.return_all_lrs, #if str($method.key_added) != '': key_added='$method.key_added', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if de_method='$method.de_method', seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, verbose=False, inplace=True) #end if
#if str($method.method) == 'bivariate': #if str($method.complex_sep) != '': if '$method.complex_sep' == '$method.xy_sep': raise ValueError('complex_sep and xy_sep must be different to keep complex and interaction names unambiguous') #end if #if $adata.ext == 'h5mu': if not '$method.mudata_options.x_mod' or not '$method.mudata_options.y_mod': raise ValueError('Both x_mod and y_mod are required for MuData bivariate analysis.') #end if result = li.method.bivariate( input_data, #if str($method.local_name) != 'None': local_name='$method.local_name', #else: local_name=None, #end if #if $method.global_name: global_name=[ #for $global_metric in $method.global_name: '$global_metric', #end for ], #else: global_name=None, #end if @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ connectivity_key='$method.connectivity_key', mask_negatives=$method.mask_negatives, add_categories=$method.add_categories, #if str($method.n_perms) != '': n_perms=$method.n_perms, #end if seed=$method.seed, nz_prop=$method.nz_prop, remove_self_interactions=$method.remove_self_interactions, #if str($method.complex_sep) != '': complex_sep='$method.complex_sep', #else: complex_sep=None, #end if xy_sep='$method.xy_sep', #if $adata.ext == 'h5mu': x_mod='$method.mudata_options.x_mod', y_mod='$method.mudata_options.y_mod', #if str($method.mudata_options.x_name) != '': x_name='$method.mudata_options.x_name', #end if #if str($method.mudata_options.y_name) != '': y_name='$method.mudata_options.y_name', #end if x_use_raw=$method.mudata_options.x_use_raw, y_use_raw=$method.mudata_options.y_use_raw, #if str($method.mudata_options.x_layer) != '': x_layer='$method.mudata_options.x_layer', #end if #if str($method.mudata_options.y_layer) != '': y_layer='$method.mudata_options.y_layer', #end if #else: #if str($method.x_name) != '': x_name='$method.x_name', #end if #if str($method.y_name) != '': y_name='$method.y_name', #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if #end if verbose=False) if result is not None: if hasattr(result, 'write_h5ad'): for column in result.var.columns: if column.endswith('_pvals') and result.var[column].isna().all(): result.var[column] = np.nan adata = result print(f"bivariate_numeric_mean={float(result.X.mean()):.8f}") else: for column in result.columns: if column.endswith('_pvals') and result[column].isna().all(): result[column] = np.nan #if $adata.ext == 'h5mu': adata = input_data.mod['$method.mudata_options.x_mod'].copy() for key in input_data.obsp: adata.obsp[key] = input_data.obsp[key].copy() #end if adata.uns['liana_bivariate_global'] = result for metric in ['morans', 'lee']: if metric in result.columns: print(f"bivariate_{metric}_first={float(result[metric].iloc[0]):.8f}") #end if
#if str($method.method) == 'cross_pcf': spatial_result = li.method.cross_pcf( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', spatial_key='$method.spatial_key', #if str($method.cell_types).strip() != '': cell_types=[x.strip() for x in '$method.cell_types'.split(',') if x.strip()], #end if min_cells=$method.min_cells, max_radius=$method.max_radius, radius_step=$method.radius_step, annulus_width=$method.annulus_width, extend_first_annulus=$method.extend_first_annulus, n_angle_samples=$method.n_angle_samples, inplace=False, verbose=False) pair_entries = list(spatial_result['results'].items()) if not pair_entries: raise ValueError('cross_pcf returned no directed cell-type pairs; lower min_cells or change cell_types') serializable_result = { 'cell_types': np.asarray(spatial_result['cell_types'], dtype=str), 'radii': np.asarray(spatial_result['radii'], dtype=float), 'source_cell_types': np.asarray([source for (source, _), _values in pair_entries], dtype=str), 'target_cell_types': np.asarray([target for (_, target), _values in pair_entries], dtype=str), 'curves': np.vstack([np.asarray(values, dtype=float) for _, values in pair_entries]), } adata.uns['$method.key_added'] = serializable_result curve_rows = [] for (source, target), values in pair_entries: for radius, value in zip(spatial_result['radii'], values): curve_rows.append({ 'method': 'cross_pcf', 'source': source, 'target': target, 'interaction': '', 'radius': radius, 'value': value }) pd.DataFrame(curve_rows).to_csv('spatial_curves.tsv', sep='\t', index=False) #end if
#if str($method.method) == 'lric': spatial_result = li.method.lric( adata=adata, @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ #if str($method.groupby).strip() != '': groupby='$method.groupby', #end if spatial_key='$method.spatial_key', max_radius=$method.max_radius, radius_step=$method.radius_step, annulus_width=$method.annulus_width, extend_first_annulus=$method.extend_first_annulus, #if str($method.groupby).strip() != '' and str($method.cell_types).strip() != '': cell_types=[x.strip() for x in '$method.cell_types'.split(',') if x.strip()], #end if min_cells=$method.min_cells, min_expressing=$method.min_expressing, n_angle_samples=$method.n_angle_samples, #if str($method.complex_sep) != '': complex_sep='$method.complex_sep', #else: complex_sep=None, #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if inplace=False, verbose=False) curve_rows = [] if 'results' in spatial_result: pair_entries = list(spatial_result['results'].items()) if not pair_entries: raise ValueError('lric returned no directed cell-type pairs; lower min_cells or change cell_types') serializable_result = { 'cell_types': np.asarray(spatial_result['cell_types'], dtype=str), 'pair_names': np.asarray(spatial_result['pair_names'], dtype=str), 'radii': np.asarray(spatial_result['radii'], dtype=float), 'source_cell_types': np.asarray([source for (source, _), _values in pair_entries], dtype=str), 'target_cell_types': np.asarray([target for (_, target), _values in pair_entries], dtype=str), 'lric': np.stack([np.asarray(matrix, dtype=float) for _, matrix in pair_entries], axis=0), } for (source, target), matrix in pair_entries: for pair_index, interaction in enumerate(spatial_result['pair_names']): for radius, value in zip(spatial_result['radii'], matrix[:, pair_index]): curve_rows.append({ 'method': 'lric', 'source': source, 'target': target, 'interaction': interaction, 'radius': radius, 'value': value }) else: serializable_result = { 'pair_names': np.asarray(spatial_result['pair_names'], dtype=str), 'radii': np.asarray(spatial_result['radii'], dtype=float), 'lric': np.asarray(spatial_result['lric'], dtype=float), } for pair_index, interaction in enumerate(spatial_result['pair_names']): for radius, value in zip(spatial_result['radii'], spatial_result['lric'][:, pair_index]): curve_rows.append({ 'method': 'lric', 'source': '', 'target': '', 'interaction': interaction, 'radius': radius, 'value': value }) adata.uns['$method.key_added'] = serializable_result pd.DataFrame(curve_rows).to_csv('spatial_curves.tsv', sep='\t', index=False) #end if
#if str($method.method) == 'compute_global_specificity': li.method.compute_global_specificity( adata=adata, groupby='$method.groupby', #if str($method.lr_sep) != '': lr_sep='$method.lr_sep', #else: lr_sep=None, #end if n_perms=$method.n_perms, seed=$method.seed, n_jobs=@N_JOBS@, use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if uns_key='$method.key_added', verbose=False) print(f"compute_global_specificity_rows={len(adata.uns['$method.key_added'])}") #end if
#if str($method.method) == 'inflow': #if str($method.complex_sep) != '': if '$method.complex_sep' == '$method.xy_sep': raise ValueError('complex_sep and xy_sep must be different to keep complex and interaction names unambiguous') #end if #if $adata.ext == 'h5mu': if not '$method.mudata_options.x_mod' or not '$method.mudata_options.y_mod': raise ValueError('Both x_mod and y_mod are required for MuData inflow analysis.') #end if result = li.method.inflow( input_data, #if str($method.celltype_selection.celltype_source) == 'groupby': groupby='$method.celltype_selection.groupby', #else: obsm_key='$method.celltype_selection.obsm_key', #end if @CMD_RESOURCE_FOR_METHODS@ nz_prop=$method.nz_prop, connectivity_key='$method.connectivity_key', #if str($method.complex_sep) != '': complex_sep='$method.complex_sep', #else: complex_sep=None, #end if use_raw=$method.use_raw, #if str($method.layer) != '': layer='$method.layer', #end if xy_sep='$method.xy_sep', #if $adata.ext == 'h5mu': x_mod='$method.mudata_options.x_mod', y_mod='$method.mudata_options.y_mod', #if str($method.mudata_options.x_name) != '': x_name='$method.mudata_options.x_name', #end if #if str($method.mudata_options.y_name) != '': y_name='$method.mudata_options.y_name', #end if x_use_raw=$method.mudata_options.x_use_raw, y_use_raw=$method.mudata_options.y_use_raw, #if str($method.mudata_options.x_layer) != '': x_layer='$method.mudata_options.x_layer', #end if #if str($method.mudata_options.y_layer) != '': y_layer='$method.mudata_options.y_layer', #end if #end if verbose=False) adata = result inflow_values = adata.X.toarray() if hasattr(adata.X, 'toarray') else np.asarray(adata.X) print(f"inflow_numeric_mean={float(np.nanmean(inflow_values)):.8f}") #end if
@CMD_ANNDATA_WRITE_OUTPUTS@

param.name: adata
param.label: Annotated data matrix
param.help: AnnData input for all methods. MuData input is accepted for bivariate and inflow analysis.

param.label: Method for ligand-receptor inference
param.option: Aggregate ligand-receptor scores from multiple methods (rank_aggregate)
Global group specificity with permutation testing
Cross pair-correlation function for directed cell-type pairs (cross_pcf)

param.name: pairs_file
param.label: Cell type pairs file
param.help: A tabular file with 'source' and 'target' columns to subset interacting cell types.

param.label: Local spatial metric
param.help: Metric for computing local (cell/spot-level) spatial associations with weighted neighbors. cosine: directional similarity; pearson/spearman: correlation-based; morans: spatial autocorrelation. None: skip local metrics.

param.label: Global spatial metrics
param.help: Select Moran's I, Lee's L, or both. Leave empty to skip global scoring.

param.label: Ligand feature label
param.help: AnnData only. Empty uses LIANA's default, ligand.

param.label: Receptor feature label
param.help: AnnData only. Empty uses LIANA's default, receptor.

param.label: Spatial connectivity matrix key
param.help: Key in adata.obsp containing the spatial connectivity/adjacency matrix. Usually created by scanpy.pp.neighbors with spatial coordinates. Default: 'spatial_connectivities'.

param.label: Exclude negative interactions
param.help: If enabled: mask low-low scores and uncategorized interactions. Useful for focusing on positive spatial associations.

param.label: Add spatial categories
param.help: If enabled: add categorical scores (e.g., high-high, high-low) based on local bivariate statistics for easier interpretation.

param.label: Permutation iterations
param.help: Leave empty to skip p-values; use 0 for the analytical Moran solution where supported; use a positive value for permutation testing.

param.label: Minimum non-zero proportion
param.help: Minimum fraction of cells where both ligand and receptor are non-zero (0-1). Default: 0.05 (5%). Filters out sparse L-R pairs.

param.label: Remove self-interactions
param.help: Remove resource rows where ligand and receptor are identical.

param.label: Interaction-name separator
param.help: Separator used when constructing ligand-receptor interaction names. It must differ from the complex separator.

param.label: Permutation iterations
param.help: Number of permutations used for empirical p-values.

expand.HELP: Key in adata.uns where the global specificity table is stored.

param.label: Cell-type matrix key
param.help: Key in adata.obsm containing a pandas DataFrame of cell-type scores or probabilities.

expand.help: Optional comma-separated subset of cell types. Empty uses all types in the groupby column.
Key in adata.uns where cross-PCF results will be stored.

param.label: Group by
param.help: Optional adata.obs cell-type column. Empty runs cell-type-agnostic LRIC.

expand.help: Optional comma-separated subset; used only when groupby is set.

param.label: Minimum expressing cells
param.help: Pairs below this ligand/receptor expressing-cell threshold are set to NaN.

expand.help: Leave empty to disable complex handling.
Key in adata.uns where LRIC results will be stored.

tool.help:
Liana: ligand-receptor inference and local bivariate spatial metrics ====================================================================
Short summary -------------
This tool provides ligand_receptor (L_R) inference methods (wrapping LIANA) and a bivariate spatial-analysis mode that computes local and global spatial association metrics for L_R pairs. The tool supports:
- Non-spatial single-cell data (scRNA-seq grouped by a `groupby` label) - Spatially-resolved transcriptomics (spot or cell-level; e.g., Visium or spatial single cells) - Hybrid workflows where a connectivity/adjacency matrix is supplied in `adata.obsp` - MuData bivariate workflows with separate ligand and receptor modalities
Available L_R methods ---------------------
**rank_aggregate**: Aggregate L_R scores across methods using RobustRankAggregate or mean-rank strategies **cellchat**: Run the CellChat L_R method **cellphonedb**: Run the CellPhoneDB method (with permutation testing) **connectome**: Run the Connectome method **natmi**: Run the NATMI method **logfc**: Log fold-change scoring **singlecellsignalr**: Run SingleCellSignalR **geometric_mean**: Geometric mean scoring method **bivariate**: Compute local and/or global bivariate spatial association metrics for L_R pairs. The returned AnnData is now used directly, while global-only DataFrame results are stored in ``adata.uns['liana_bivariate_global']``:
- Local metrics (per observation): cosine, jaccard, pearson, spearman, masked_spearman, product, norm_product, morans - Global metrics (per pair): morans (Moran's I) and lee (Lee's L), selectable individually or together - Optional categorical summarization (`add_categories`) produces interpretable labels (e.g., high_high, high_low) - `mask_negatives` can be used to ignore negative/low_low interactions
**compute_global_specificity**: Compute group-specific interaction means and permutation-based empirical p-values using Galaxy-allocated CPU slots.
**cross_pcf**: Compute distance-resolved cross pair-correlation curves for every directed sender-to-receiver cell-type pair using spatial coordinates only.
**inflow**: Compute trivariate source-cell-type, ligand, and receptor scores over a spatial connectivity graph.
**lric**: Compute expression-weighted cross-PCF curves for ligand-receptor pairs, globally or stratified by directed cell-type pairs.
Both new methods write a serializable result to ``adata.uns[key_added]`` and emit a long-form ``spatial_curves.tsv`` table for downstream plotting. Directed cell-type pairs are stored as separate source and target arrays, avoiding delimiter-dependent pair encoding.
Key parameters (common) -----------------------
**groupby**: Column in `adata.obs` that defines cell identities/groups
**Resource selection**: priority: interactions > resource > resource_name
1. `interactions` — explicit list of "ligand,receptor" pairs 2. `resource` — user tabular file with `ligand` and `receptor` columns 3. `resource_name` — built-in resource (default `consensus`)
**expr_prop**: Minimum fraction of cells in a group expressing the gene; set `0` to disable **min_cells**: Minimum number of cells per identity **use_raw / layer**: Choose expression source: `adata.raw.X`, `adata.layers[...]`, or `adata.X` **de_method, n_perms, seed**: Differential expression method, permutation count (if applicable), and RNG seed
Bivariate-specific parameters -----------------------------
**local_name**: Choose the local metric (or `None` to skip local scoring) **global_name**: Select `morans`, `lee`, or both. Leave the selection empty to skip global scoring. **connectivity_key**: Key in `adata.obsp` holding the adjacency/connectivity matrix (default `spatial_connectivities`) **mask_negatives**: When `True`, ignore negative/low_low local scores **add_categories**: When `True`, produce categorical labels per observation from local scores **nz_prop**: Minimum fraction of observations where both ligand and receptor are non-zero (default `0.05`)
Outputs -------
- L_R inference methods write results to `adata.uns['liana_res']` (or `key_added` when provided) - Bivariate local analyses return an AnnData whose matrix contains local scores; global statistics are in ``.var``. Global-only results are stored in ``adata.uns['liana_bivariate_global']``. - cross-PCF and LRIC write serializable dictionaries to ``adata.uns[key_added]`` and also output a long-form spatial-curves table.
